summary: Small rectangular single-storey building with brick walls, a stepped dark nether-brick gable roof, white trim belt and a raised white border around a shallow surrounding trench; windows run along the long sides and stone corner pillars accent corners.

shape.overall_form: rectangular footprint with raised perimeter trim and recessed ground level (shallow trench) surrounding it
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis

elements: long rows of windows on both long sides (three or four panes each segment)
stepped dark roof with projecting stone slab caps at each step
raised white border/low wall around building creating a recessed trench
rebuild_hints: Footprint is rectangular; use a white (quartz) 1-block-wide border set outward from the brick wall and a 1-block-deep trench around the border.
Walls: brick main body with a single row of plank skirting at the base and stone pillars at each corner that rise to meet the white belt under the roof.
Windows: continuous repeating window sections along long sides just above the plank skirting and below the white belt; replicate spacing seen (approximately 4 window segments per long side).
Roof: build a symmetrical stepped gable using nether_brick stairs/blocks in ascending layers toward centerline; place stone (or slab_stone) caps at the ends of each step and a central ridge.
Height: keep overall building low (single storey) — walls roughly 3 blocks tall above the plank skirting, belt layer, then 2–3 stepped roof layers.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white trim and corner accents: white appears to be quartz but could be another white block; corner blocks look like stonebrick or cobblestone variants.
Precise internal layout and whether there is an entrance on a side not shown (no visible door in provided views).
Exact counts for windows and interior floor height; estimates based only on exterior visible blocks and may be off by 1–2 blocks.
